annotations.label: Five lemma (informal)

anchor.snippet: the five lemma is an important and widely used lemma about commutative diagrams

annotations.note: The five lemma is formalized as a theorem in Mathlib for abelian categories (and unbundled for groups/modules in Mathlib.Algebra.FiveLemma).

annotations.label: Five lemma

anchor.snippet: The five lemma states that, if the rows are exact

annotations.note: Stated for `ComposableArrows C 4` in an abelian category as `isIso_of_epi_of_isIso_of_isIso_of_mono`, matching the Wikipedia hypotheses (m epi, l/p iso, q mono ⟹ n iso).

annotations.label: First four lemma (epimorphism)

anchor.snippet: If the rows in the commutative diagram are exact and m and p are epimorphisms and q is a monomorphism, then n is an epimorphism

annotations.note: `epi_of_epi_of_epi_of_mono` for `ComposableArrows C 3` in an abelian category states exactly this four-lemma; an unbundled module version is `LinearMap.surjective_of_surjective_of_surjective_of_injective`.

annotations.label: Second four lemma (monomorphism)

anchor.snippet: If the rows in the commutative diagram are exact and m and p are monomorphisms and l is an epimorphism, then n is a monomorphism

annotations.note: `mono_of_epi_of_mono_of_mono` for `ComposableArrows C 3` states the mono version; an unbundled module version is `LinearMap.injective_of_surjective_of_injective_of_injective`.

annotations.label: n is surjective (proof of four lemma 1)
annotations.note: The conclusion of the surjectivity half of the four-lemma diagram chase is precisely the statement of `epi_of_epi_of_epi_of_mono` (its proof in Mathlib follows the same diagram-chase argument).

annotations.label: n is injective (proof of four lemma 2)
annotations.note: The conclusion of the injectivity half of the four-lemma diagram chase is precisely the statement of `mono_of_epi_of_mono_of_mono`.

annotations.label: Application to long exact sequences

anchor.snippet: The five lemma is often applied to long exact sequences

annotations.note: This is a methodological remark about how the five lemma is applied to long exact sequences, not a specific theorem; no corresponding meta-lemma is formalized.

annotations.label: Comparison of (co)homology theories

anchor.snippet: it is quite helpful in proving two (co)homology theories of the same object coincide

annotations.note: A general application remark about comparing (co)homology theories; no theorem of this form is stated in Mathlib.
